title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Execution Trace: Cleanup Compensation
summary: A request starts database and outbound work with background contexts, ignores disconnects, exhausts its entire deadline before cleanup, and leaks goroutines after middleware returns. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo cleanup compensation increment in deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.
Give every request goroutine a stop path, bounded result channel, single closer, retained error, and join or deliberate ownership transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Cleanup Compensation · Theory Cleanup Compensation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7660 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cleanup Compensation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Go server Predict Cleanup Compensation · Theory Cleanup Compensation.

For Go server Predict Cleanup Compensation · Theory Cleanup Compensation, keep Cleanup Compensation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
